steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Critics say limitarianism is ______ because it requires inter-government cooperation that is "likely unachievable." The blank must capture something that is idealistic but impractical.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘perfunctory’: Means carried out with minimal effort or as a routine duty. The critics are not saying limitarianism is careless — they're saying it's unrealistic. Wrong meaning. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘utopian’: Means idealistic and impractical, impossible to achieve in reality. This directly captures the critics' claim: limitarianism requires cooperation that is "likely unachievable." ✓
  • Choice C — ‘punitive’: Means intended as punishment. The critics' argument is about feasibility, not whether limitarianism is punishing someone. Unrelated. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘viable’: Means capable of working. This contradicts the critics' point entirely — they argue it is not achievable. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
